title: euphrasia officinalis subsp. coerulea
intro: Euphrasia officinalis subsp. coerulea, also known as blue-eyed grass or blue-flowered eyebright, is a subspecies of the flowering plant Euphrasia officinalis. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves, stems, and flowers. The plant contains bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, offering various health benefits such as soothing eye irritations, reducing inflammation, and promoting respiratory health. Euphrasia officinalis subsp. coerulea is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to treat various conditions, including eye problems, coughs, and colds.
